title: SQG Identity: A Lean 4 Formalization of Fourier-Space Identities for the Surface Quasi-Geostrophic Equation with Conditional Regularity Roadmap
description: A Lean 4 + mathlib formalization of Fourier-space identities for the inviscid Surface Quasi-Geostrophic equation on the 2-torus, together with a conditional regularity roadmap. Two theorems are machine-verified unconditionally: the shear-vorticity identity (the combination S_nt − ω/2 has Fourier multiplier |k|·sin²(α−β) and vanishes identically for one-dimensional fronts) and the selection-rule bound (pointwise mode-level inequality with equality characterization, lifted to an integrated Sobolev-seminorm form via Parseval). Supporting infrastructure includes Riesz transforms on the torus via Fourier multipliers, Leray–Helmholtz projection, the fractional Sobolev scale, a self-adjoint Biot–Savart factorization, tight mode-level identities for the strain and vorticity tensors, and an α-fractional heat semigroup with smoothing and contractivity bounds plus a parallel suite for the classical heat semigroup. A conditional form of the SQG regularity theorem is formalized in §10 as named Lean structures for the fractional Sobolev calculus, material maximum principle, BKM criterion, and SQG evolution axioms; uniform Sobolev bounds follow from these hypotheses. The fractional Sobolev calculus is discharged unconditionally from mathlib. On the class of trajectories with finite Fourier support, real-coefficient symmetry, and a uniform L^∞ coefficient bound, both analytic axioms are discharged: the material maximum principle directly, and the BKM criterion via a Kato–Ponce commutator bound, an advection-cancellation theorem for the Fourier energy derivative, and a scalar Gronwall argument, so that regularity on the Sobolev range s ∈ [0, 2] is unconditional on this class. Extensions in v0.4.10 through v0.4.37 carry the conditional chain forward to an honest L²(𝕋²) solution object: §10.116 delivers unconditional time-global existence of the Galerkin ODE trajectory on every symmetric Fourier support from any real-symmetric ℓ²-bounded initial coefficient vector, discharging ball-invariance, real-symmetry propagation, and ℓ²-sum conservation internally; §10.117 and §10.132 package this trajectory as an honest SqgSolution on L²(𝕋²) via two parallel routes (direct assembly and hypothesis-keyed Galerkin-limit chain §10.125–§10.130); §10.133–§10.134 upgrade the result to the Duhamel-level SqgEvolutionAxioms_strong through an Ici-0 port of the §10.91–§10.94 chain; §10.135–§10.136 structurally close the time-test → Duhamel bridge; and §10.137–§10.146 establish a Route B conditional chain that reduces generic-L² Galerkin → SqgSolution extraction to two named classical analytical inputs (an Aubin–Lions extraction witness from the uniform L² bound and an H⁻² time-derivative bound, and an L²-norm continuity datum composed with Galerkin ℓ²-conservation), exercised unconditionally on the zero datum. The release comprises 20,137 lines of Lean 4 source with zero `sorry` and no axioms beyond mathlib; the build is reproducible with a single `lake build` against mathlib v4.29.0. The accompanying paper deriving the identity, selection rule, and conditional regularity theorem is distributed with the release.
